highlight.title: MCP Security Crisis: 53% of Servers Use Hard-Coded Credentials
highlight.summary: <cite index='11-32,11-28'>Research analyzing over 5,000 unique open-source MCP server implementations found that 53% use insecure hard-coded credentials, with approximately 88% requiring credentials</cite>. <cite index='15-13,15-14'>Major vulnerabilities like CVE-2025-6514 have compromised over 437,000 developer environments, giving attackers access to environment variables, credentials, and internal repositories</cite>.

resources.title: MCP November 2025 Specification: Tasks and Enterprise Features
resources.why_now: <cite index='21-16,21-17'>The November 2025 spec introduces experimental async tasks for long-running workflows like healthcare data analysis and enterprise automation, marking MCP's evolution from simple tool calls to production-ready infrastructure</cite>.

resources.title: State of MCP Server Security 2025 Research Report
resources.why_now: <cite index='11-20,11-21'>With over 16,000 MCP servers now indexed and the Astrix team releasing an open-source MCP Secret Wrapper to address credential management issues, security tooling is finally catching up to MCP's explosive growth</cite>.

resources.title: Chrome DevTools MCP Server Gets Auto-Connection Feature
resources.why_now: <cite index='22-7,22-8'>The v0.12.1 DevTools MCP server now supports auto-connection, enabling developers to reuse existing browser sessions and pick up debugging where they left off, streamlining the development workflow</cite>.

resources.title: JetBrains AI Pulse Survey: Claude Code Reaches 18% Developer Adoption
resources.why_now: <cite index='36-12,36-14'>Claude Code's adoption jumped from 3% to 18% in under a year, now tied with Cursor for second place behind GitHub Copilot, with the highest satisfaction (91% CSAT) and recommendation (54 NPS) scores in the market</cite>.

claude_take: MCP is hitting a critical inflection point where security practices must mature alongside adoption—the protocol's rapid evolution from experimental tool to enterprise infrastructure demands immediate attention to credential management, authentication, and supply chain security. In 3-6 months, expect MCP to become the standard plumbing for multi-agent systems, but only organizations that invest in security tooling and governance frameworks now will be positioned to deploy safely at scale.
